message:
franken_numpy-ifm0 add polynomial power-basis helper wrappers

Add four numpy.polynomial.polynomial passthrough wrappers to fnp-python
that were missing per POLYNOMIAL_COMPLIANCE.generated.md:

- polyline(off, scl): degree-1 polynomial constructor
- polytrim(c, tol=0.0): trim trailing near-zero coefficients
- polyvalfromroots(x, r, tensor=true): evaluate polynomial from its roots
- polyvander(x, deg): Vandermonde matrix in the power basis

Each wrapper imports numpy.polynomial.polynomial at call time and forwards
arguments/kwargs directly so call surfaces stay byte-for-byte compatible
with the upstream NumPy API.

Conformance tests in crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs compare every wrapper
against numpy.polynomial.polynomial:
- polyline: covers (1,-1), (0,0), (-2.5,3.25)
- polytrim: covers default tol, tol=1e-5 truncation, all-zero coeffs
- polyvalfromroots: 1-D points + scalar roots, plus 2-D root matrix with
  tensor=False (broadcast against paired points)
- polyvander: deg=0/1/4 plus error-surface check on deg=-1 (verifies the
  same exception type bubbles up from NumPy)

Bead bookkeeping: marks ifm0 in_progress and registers two follow-up
PORT beads (nys5 basis-conversion helpers, ur9y orthogonal Vandermonde
helpers) for the remaining polynomial parity gaps surfaced by the same
reality-check pass.
